bugs.title: Duplicate itinerary items created for day trips
bugs.description: When asked for day trip recommendations, the AI called create_itinerary_items twice in the same response — once before emitting the text response and once after. This resulted in duplicate entries on Day 4 (two Montserrat items) and Day 5 (two Girona/Dali items). The dedup logic caught the second attempt for restaurant items (returned already_exists), but the day trip duplicates persisted because the titles were slightly different.
bugs.steps_to_reproduce: Ask the AI for day trip recommendations in planning mode. The AI calls create_itinerary_items, then generates text with the affiliate link, then calls create_itinerary_items again with slightly different titles for the same activities.
bugs.expected: Each day trip should appear once in the itinerary.
bugs.actual: Day 4 has 'Day Trip: Montserrat Monastery & Mountains' AND 'Day Trip to Montserrat Monastery'. Day 5 has 'Girona & Dali Museum Day Trip' AND 'Day Trip: Girona & Dali Museum in Figueres'.

bugs.title: AI response content duplication in recommend_booking flow
bugs.description: When the AI called recommend_booking for day trips, the full response contained duplicate content — the AI described the two day trips, provided the affiliate link with disclosure, then repeated very similar descriptions of the same two day trips after the tool_result continuation. The messageComplete fullContent shows the repetition clearly.
bugs.expected: A single coherent response without repeated paragraphs.
bugs.actual: The response repeats the Montserrat and Girona descriptions twice, once before and once after the affiliate link section.

ux_issues.description: Three restaurant options were all added to the itinerary as separate items on Day 2, all with order_in_day=3. For an organized user like Priya, seeing three dinner options cluttering the itinerary instead of one chosen restaurant is confusing. The AI should present options in text but only add a confirmed choice to the itinerary, or mark them as 'options' somehow.
ux_issues.suggestion: Add an itinerary item type like 'option' or only add restaurant items to itinerary when the user confirms a choice.

ai_behavior_issues.issue: AI called create_itinerary_items twice in the same response for day trip recommendations, creating duplicates with slightly different titles
ai_behavior_issues.context: The tool loop caused the AI to generate items, emit text, then generate the same items again in the continuation turn. The dedup logic caught exact title matches (restaurants) but missed near-matches (day trips).

ai_behavior_issues.issue: Response duplication in multi-turn tool loop: AI repeated day trip descriptions verbatim after the recommend_booking tool result continuation
ai_behavior_issues.context: The completeResponse combined both the pre-tool and post-tool text, resulting in duplicated content visible to the user.

usefulness_evaluation.narrative: Booking ingestion and retrieval worked flawlessly — all three bookings were parsed correctly with accurate field extraction. The AI answered specific booking questions (terminal, check-in time) accurately from ingested data. The recommend_booking tool triggered correctly for bookable day trips and did NOT trigger for restaurant suggestions. FTC affiliate disclosure was present. Companion mode responses were concise and practical. The main issues were duplicate itinerary items from the tool loop creating items twice, and response content duplication in the recommend_booking flow. The itinerary quality suffered from these duplicates making it look cluttered rather than clean.
